economic_notes: Bear River First Nation owns 75% of Digby Pines Resorts Limited Partnership (operating as Digby Pines Golf Resort and Spa), accounted for on modified equity basis. Also operates L'sitkuk Gas Bar Limited and holds fishing lobster and tuna licenses through Fisheries segment. Subsidiaries include Bear River First Nation Housing Projects, Bear River First Nation Health Center, L'sitkuk Gas Bar Limited, 3331786 Nova Scotia Limited, and 3331787 Nova Scotia Limited.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2021, not 2024. Province is Nova Scotia (NS). The budget was approved May 9, 2017 which is unusually old. Digby Pines Golf Resort and Spa (75% owned) is accounted for on modified equity basis as a government business enterprise due to shared control with an unrelated private party. The consolidated totals in the segment disclosure show total revenue of $6,071,936 which differs from the statement of operations total of $5,830,691 — the segment schedule appears to include gross Digby Pines revenues not reflected in the consolidated statement. Investment income is negative (loss) of $218,276. No schedule of remuneration was included in the financial statements provided. Annual debt service reflects principal repayments only per cash flow statement ($98,298). New long-term debt of $282,973 was issued during the year including a $200,000 Peace Hills Trust loan.
